questionBank.question: If f is continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), MVT guarantees:
questionBank.explanation: This is the exact MVT statement.
questionBank.notesSnippet: MVT links average rate to an instantaneous rate.

questionBank.question: If f'(x)>0 on an interval, f is:
questionBank.explanation: Positive derivative means function is increasing.

questionBank.question: A relative max can occur where:
questionBank.explanation: Max occurs when slope switches from positive to negative.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Use first derivative sign changes at critical points.
questionBank.timeTip: Track sign before and after c.

questionBank.question: If f''(x)<0 on an interval, graph of f is:
questionBank.explanation: Negative second derivative means concave down.

questionBank.question: To maximize area with constraints, best AP strategy is:

choices.B: Set derivative equal to 0 after reducing to one variable

questionBank.explanation: Optimization: model objective in one variable, then critical points.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Build objective function then analyze derivative.

frqBank.title: Unit 5 FRQ - Graph Analysis and Optimization

parts.label: (a)
parts.question: Find critical points of f.
parts.scoring: 1 pt derivative, 1 pt equation, 1 pt roots.

parts.label: (b)
parts.question: Classify each critical point.
parts.finalAnswer: x=1 is local max, x=3 is local min
steps: f' changes + to - at x=1 => max.
f' changes - to + at x=3 => min.
parts.scoring: 1 pt sign method, 1 pt x=1 class, 1 pt x=3 class.

5.1.title: 5-1 to 5-5 Extrema and MVT
5.1.content:
Critical-point workflow

Find f', solve f'=0/undefined, then classify using sign changes.

5.2.title: 5-6 Concavity + 2nd Derivative Test
5.2.content:
Concavity

f''>0 concave up, f''<0 concave down. Inflection where concavity changes.

5.3.title: 5-10 Optimization
5.3.content:
Optimization strategy

Objective function -> one variable -> derivative -> test candidates.
